name: Butter Bean, Mushroom & Walnut Loaf
description: A deeply savoury vegetarian centrepiece loaf of butter beans, chestnut and porcini mushrooms, and toasted walnuts — slices cleanly at the table.

ingredients.name: dried porcini mushrooms
ingredients.notes: soaked in 150ml just-boiled water for at least 20 minutes; reserve the soaking liquid

ingredients.name: oats
ingredients.notes: optional; add 1 tbsp if mixture is very wet before filling the tin. Not listed in source ingredient list — added from method

steps.title: Soak the porcini
steps.content: Place {0003} in a small bowl and cover with 150ml of just-boiled water. Set aside for at least 20 minutes. Drain and reserve the soaking liquid for another use. Roughly chop the soaked porcini.

steps.title: Toast the walnuts
steps.content: Toast {0006} in a dry frying pan over medium heat until fragrant and just starting to colour. Set aside.

steps.title: Cook the onion and garlic
steps.content: Heat {0008} in a large frying pan over medium-low heat. Add {0004}, {0010} and {0011} and cook gently until the onion is completely soft. Add {0005} and cook for a further 2 minutes.

steps.title: Cook down the mushrooms
steps.content: Add {0002} and turn the heat up to medium-high. Cook, stirring occasionally, until all moisture has evaporated and the mushrooms are deeply coloured and almost dry. Add the chopped {0003} and {0009}. Season well with {0012} and {0013}. Cook for 2 more minutes, then set aside to cool slightly.

steps.title: Prepare the tin
steps.content: Preheat oven to 200°C/180°C fan. Line a 900g loaf tin with baking parchment and brush with {0014}.

steps.title: Make the filling
steps.content: Tip {0001} into a large bowl and roughly mash — mostly smooth but with some texture remaining. Add the mushroom mixture, {0006} and {0007}. Mix thoroughly. Taste and adjust seasoning with {0012} and {0013}. If the mixture feels very wet, stir in {0015}.

steps.title: Fill the tin
steps.content: Spoon the mixture into the prepared tin, pressing it down firmly and smoothing the top.

steps.title: Roast
steps.content: Roast for 35–40 minutes until the top is firm and well coloured.

steps.title: Rest and turn out
steps.content: Leave in the tin for 10 minutes before turning out. Turn onto a board, remove the parchment, and slice with a sharp serrated knife.

notes:
The porcini soaking liquid is worth keeping — strain through a fine sieve and refrigerate for up to 3 days or freeze. It makes an excellent base for a porcini and red wine miso gravy.

Don't over-mash the beans — some texture makes for a better slice and a more interesting bite alongside the walnut chunks.

The filling can be made up to a day ahead and kept in the fridge; spoon into the tin and roast when needed.

_fixture_notes.external_ref: Supplied slug 'butter-bean-mushroom-walnut-loaf' used verbatim; not re-derived.
_fixture_notes.nutritional_tags: Source states 'Fits the omega 3/6 strategy throughout' — explicit prose equivalent of omega3_strong per the spec. Tagged accordingly.
_fixture_notes.cook_time_minutes: No labelled cook field. Stated step durations: porcini soak 20 min (in-session passive, ≤2 hours); garlic cook 2 min; porcini/tamari finish 2 min; roast 35 min (lower bound). Concurrency: porcini soak (step 1) runs while steps 2–4 proceed — source structure implies this. Steps 2–4 combined include unstated durations (walnut toast, onion softening). Critical path of the parallel block cannot be determined confidently — onion step has no stated duration and is likely the longest element. Both timing fields left null. Individual step timers capture the 20-min soak, 2-min garlic, 2-min mushroom finish, and 35-min roast.
_fixture_notes.prep_time_minutes: No labelled prep field. Left null.
_fixture_notes.post_cook_rest: Step 9 (rest in tin 10 min) is post-cook rest — excluded from cook_time_minutes per spec. Timer null.
_fixture_notes.salt_and_pepper: Source lists 'salt and black pepper' as a single combined line. Combined-seasonings convention applied: split into two rows (0012 salt, 0013 black pepper).
_fixture_notes.olive_oil_duplicate: Olive oil appears in two distinct uses: 3 tbsp for cooking (0008) and 1 tbsp for greasing the tin (0014). Separate rows per duplicate-ingredient-across-groups convention.
_fixture_notes.tamari_alternative: Source lists 'tamari or soy sauce'. Inline-alternatives convention: tamari canonical (first-named), soy sauce noted.
_fixture_notes.butter_beans_total: Source lists '2 tins butter beans (400g each), drained and rinsed'. Tin weight convention: amount set to 800g (2 × 400g), format noted. Using total weight as the shopping quantity is the cleaner representation; tin count and format in notes.
_fixture_notes.oats_method_only: Step 6 mentions 'stir in a tablespoon of oats' as a conditional fix if the mixture is very wet. Not in the source ingredient list. Added as row 0015 with amount null, unit null, per the method-only optionals convention.
_fixture_notes.porcini_referenced_twice: Dried porcini (0003) is referenced in step 1 (soak and chop) and step 4 (add to mushrooms). One row, two step references — correct. The soaked/chopped form used in step 4 is the same ingredient prepared in step 1.
_fixture_notes.walnuts_referenced_twice: Walnuts (0006) are toasted in step 2 and added to the filling in step 6. One row, two step references — correct.
_fixture_notes.dried_rosemary: 'Dried rosemary, crushed' — 'crushed' is a prep verb. Source already uses trailing form; convention satisfied. 'Dried' is canonical-identity modifier, stays.
_fixture_notes.dietary_restrictions: Eggs: eggs. Nuts: walnuts. Soy: tamari (canonical choice contains soy). Oats (optional, row 0015) are not in the spec's restriction code list. No dairy, gluten (tamari is typically gluten-free; no wheat ingredients), sesame, shellfish, or peanuts.
_fixture_notes.cuisine: British-style vegetarian centrepiece loaf with no strong single-cuisine identity. Left null.
_fixture_notes.no_source: Source is an operator-written document with no external attribution. source field left null.
_fixture_notes.no_derived_components: Batch mode — no derived components proposed. No obvious reusable component candidates in a single-piece loaf.
